entries.name: isClientFirebaseError
entries.description:
Type guard that checks whether an error is a client-side {@link FirebaseError} from the `firebase/app` package.

Distinguishes client Firebase errors from server-side errors or generic errors by checking
for the `FirebaseError` name and a string `code` property. Used by {@link convertHttpsCallableErrorToReadableError}
to determine the appropriate error wrapping strategy.

params.name: error
params.description: - The value to check.

entries.returns: `true` if the input matches the shape of a client-side `FirebaseError`

entries.name: FirebaseServerError
entries.description:
Client-side representation of an error originating from a Firebase Cloud Function.

Wraps a {@link FirebaseError} with structured server error details (status, message, code)
extracted from the error's `details` property. Extends {@link ServerErrorResponse} for
consistent error handling across the application.

Typically created via {@link convertHttpsCallableErrorToReadableError} when an `HttpsCallable`
invocation fails with a server-side error that includes structured details.
entries.returns: FirebaseServerError

entries.name: convertHttpsCallableErrorToReadableError

params.name: error
params.description: - The caught error from an `HttpsCallable` invocation.

entries.returns: A {@link FirebaseServerError } if the error has structured details, or a generic readable error otherwise.

entries.name: firebaseAuthErrorToReadableError
entries.description:
Converts a {@link FirebaseAuthError} into a user-friendly {@link ReadableError} with a human-readable message.

Maps known error codes (wrong password, user not found, network errors) to contextual messages
suitable for display in the UI.

params.name: inputError
params.description: - The Firebase Auth error to convert.

entries.returns: A {@link ReadableError } with a human-readable message suitable for display.

entries.name: FIREBASE_AUTH_OOB_CODE_DATA_PAIR_DELIMITER
entries.description:
Delimiter used when encoding a {@link FirebaseAuthOobCodeDataPair} into a {@link FirebaseAuthOobCode}.

The encoded form is `${code}-${uid}` and splitting happens on the first delimiter occurrence,
so this only requires that the generated code/password not contain `-`. The uid is unrestricted
and may itself contain `-`.

entries.name: encodeFirebaseAuthOobCode
entries.description:
Encodes a {@link FirebaseAuthOobCodeDataPair} into a URL-safe {@link FirebaseAuthOobCode}.

The encoded form is `${code}-${uid}` — code first so that splitting on the first delimiter
still works even when the uid itself contains `-` (Firebase uids occasionally do).
entries.returns: FirebaseAuthOobCode

entries.name: decodeFirebaseAuthOobCode
entries.description:
Decodes a {@link FirebaseAuthOobCode} back into its {@link FirebaseAuthOobCodeDataPair}.

Splits on the first `-` so uids containing additional `-` characters survive the round-trip
intact. Returns null when the input is missing the delimiter or has empty parts.
entries.returns: Maybe<FirebaseAuthOobCodeDataPair>

entries.name: newDocuments
entries.signature: newDocuments(documentAccessor: FirestoreDocumentAccessor<T, D>, count: number): D[]
entries.description:
Creates an array of new {@link FirestoreDocument} instances without persisting them to Firestore.

Each document is allocated a unique auto-generated ID via {@link FirestoreDocumentAccessor.newDocument},
but no data is written to the database. Useful for preparing document references in bulk before
deciding what data to write.

params.name: documentAccessor
params.description: - Accessor that provides the `newDocument()` factory method.

params.name: count
params.description: - Number of document instances to create.

entries.returns: Array of `count` new document instances, each with a unique auto-generated ID.

entries.name: makeDocuments
entries.signature: makeDocuments(documentAccessor: FirestoreDocumentAccessor<T, D>, make: MakeDocumentsParams<T, D>): Promise<D[]>
entries.description:
Creates and optionally persists multiple Firestore documents in sequence.

Uses {@link performMakeLoop} to iterate `count` times. For each iteration:
1. A new document instance is created (via `make.newDocument` or the default factory)
2. `make.init(i, document)` is awaited to produce initial data
3. If init returns non-nullish data, `document.accessor.create(data)` persists it
4. The document instance is collected regardless of whether it was persisted

Documents are created sequentially (not in parallel) to allow index-dependent logic.

params.name: documentAccessor
params.description: - Accessor providing the document factory and collection context.

params.name: make
params.description: - Configuration controlling count, factory, and initialization.

entries.returns: Promise resolving to all created document instances (length === `make.count`)

entries.name: getDocumentSnapshots
entries.description:
Fetches {@link DocumentSnapshot}s for multiple documents in parallel using {@link runAsyncTasksForValues}.

Each document's `accessor.get()` is called concurrently. The returned array preserves
the same ordering as the input `documents` array.

params.name: documents
params.description: - Documents to fetch snapshots for.

entries.returns: Snapshots in the same order as the input array.

entries.name: getDocumentSnapshotPair
entries.description: Fetches the current snapshot of a single document and pairs it with the document instance.

params.name: document
params.description: - The document to fetch.

entries.returns: A pair containing the document and its snapshot.

entries.name: getDocumentSnapshotPairs
entries.description: Fetches snapshots for multiple documents in parallel and pairs each with its document instance.

params.name: documents
params.description: - Documents to fetch.

entries.returns: Pairs in the same order as the input array.

entries.name: getDocumentSnapshotDataPair
entries.description: Fetches a document's snapshot, extracts its data with `id`/`key` fields, and returns all three as a triplet.

params.name: document
params.description: - The document to fetch.

entries.returns: A triplet of document, snapshot, and data (data may be `undefined` if the document doesn't exist)

entries.name: getDocumentSnapshotDataPairs
entries.description:
Fetches snapshot-data triplets for multiple documents in parallel.

Processes up to 200 documents concurrently via {@link runAsyncTasksForValues}.
The returned array preserves the same ordering as the input.

params.name: documents
params.description: - Documents to fetch.

entries.returns: Triplets in the same order as the input array.

entries.name: getDocumentSnapshotDataPairsWithData
entries.description:
Fetches snapshot-data triplets for multiple documents and filters out those that don't exist in Firestore.

Convenience wrapper around {@link getDocumentSnapshotDataPairs} that removes entries where `data` is nullish,
returning only {@link FirestoreDocumentSnapshotDataPairWithData} results.

params.name: documents
params.description: - Documents to fetch.

entries.returns: Triplets for documents that exist, in their original relative order.

entries.name: getDocumentSnapshotDataTuples
entries.description:
Fetches raw snapshot data tuples for multiple documents in parallel.

Unlike {@link getDocumentSnapshotDataPairs}, this returns a lightweight `[document, data]` tuple
and does not inject `id`/`key` fields onto the data. The `data` value is the raw result of
`snapshot.data()` and may be `undefined` for non-existent documents.

params.name: documents
params.description: - Documents to fetch.

entries.returns: Tuples in the same order as the input array.

entries.name: getDocumentSnapshotData
entries.description:
Fetches the data from a single document's snapshot.

By default (`withId=true`), the returned data has `id` and `key` fields injected via
{@link documentDataWithIdAndKey}. Pass `withId=false` to get the raw snapshot data instead.

Returns `undefined` if the document does not exist.

params.name: document
params.description: - The document to fetch data for

params.name: withId
params.description: - Whether to inject `id` and `key` fields onto the data (default: `true`)

entries.returns: The document's data, or `undefined` if it doesn't exist

entries.name: getDocumentSnapshotsData
entries.description:
Fetches data for multiple documents in parallel, filtering out non-existent documents.

By default (`withId=true`), each data object has `id` and `key` fields injected.
Documents that don't exist in Firestore are excluded from the results (the returned
array may be shorter than the input).

params.name: documents
params.description: - Documents to fetch data for

params.name: withId
params.description: - Whether to inject `id` and `key` fields onto each data object (default: `true`)

entries.returns: Data objects for existing documents only

entries.name: getDataFromDocumentSnapshots
entries.signature: getDataFromDocumentSnapshots(snapshots: DocumentSnapshot<T>[], withId?: boolean): DocumentDataWithIdAndKey<T>[] | T[]
entries.description:
Extracts data from an array of {@link DocumentSnapshot}s, filtering out snapshots for non-existent documents.

By default (`withId=true`), each data object has `id` and `key` fields injected via {@link documentDataWithIdAndKey}.
Snapshots where `data()` returns `undefined` are removed from the output via {@link filterMaybeArrayValues}.

params.name: snapshots
params.description: - Snapshots to extract data from

params.name: withId
params.description: - Whether to inject `id` and `key` fields onto each data object (default: `true`)

entries.returns: Data objects for existing documents only (may be shorter than input)

entries.name: loadDocumentsForSnapshots
entries.signature: loadDocumentsForSnapshots(accessor: LimitedFirestoreDocumentAccessor<T, D>, snapshots: QuerySnapshot<T>): D[]
entries.description:
Creates {@link FirestoreDocument} instances for all documents in a {@link QuerySnapshot}.

Maps each document in `snapshots.docs` to a loaded document via `accessor.loadDocument(ref)`.
No additional data fetching occurs; the documents are loaded from their existing references.

params.name: accessor
params.description: - Accessor to load documents with.

params.name: snapshots
params.description: - Query snapshot containing the document references.

entries.returns: Document instances in the same order as the query results.

entries.name: loadDocumentsForDocumentReferencesFromValues
entries.signature: loadDocumentsForDocumentReferencesFromValues(accessor: LimitedFirestoreDocumentAccessor<T, D>, values: I[], getRef: (value: I) => DocumentReference<T>): D[]
entries.description:
Extracts {@link DocumentReference}s from arbitrary values and loads them as {@link FirestoreDocument} instances.

Convenience function that maps values through `getRef` then delegates to {@link loadDocumentsForDocumentReferences}.

params.name: accessor
params.description: - Accessor to load documents with.

params.name: values
params.description: - Source values to extract references from.

params.name: getRef
params.description: - Extracts a document reference from each value.

entries.returns: Document instances in the same order as the input values.

entries.name: loadDocumentsForDocumentReferences
entries.description:
Loads {@link FirestoreDocument} instances from an array of {@link DocumentReference}s.

Each reference is passed to `accessor.loadDocument()` to create a document wrapper.
No network calls are made; this only creates in-memory document instances bound to the given references.

params.name: accessor
params.description: - Accessor to load documents with.

params.name: refs
params.description: - Document references to load.

entries.returns: Document instances in the same order as the input references.

entries.name: loadDocumentsForKeysFromValues
entries.signature: loadDocumentsForKeysFromValues(accessor: LimitedFirestoreDocumentAccessor<T, D>, values: I[], getKey: (value: I) => FirestoreModelKey): D[]
entries.description:
Extracts {@link FirestoreModelKey}s from arbitrary values and loads them as {@link FirestoreDocument} instances.

Convenience function that maps values through `getKey` then delegates to {@link loadDocumentsForKeys}.

params.name: accessor
params.description: - Accessor to load documents with.

params.name: values
params.description: - Source values to extract keys from.

params.name: getKey
params.description: - Extracts a model key (full Firestore path) from each value.

entries.returns: Document instances in the same order as the input values.

entries.name: loadDocumentsForKeys
entries.signature: loadDocumentsForKeys(accessor: LimitedFirestoreDocumentAccessor<T, D>, keys: FirestoreModelKey[]): D[]
entries.description:
Loads {@link FirestoreDocument} instances from an array of full Firestore document paths (keys).

Each key is passed to `accessor.loadDocumentForKey()`. No network calls are made.

params.name: accessor
params.description: - Accessor to load documents with.

params.name: keys
params.description: - Full Firestore document paths (e.g. `'users/abc123'`)

entries.returns: Document instances in the same order as the input keys.

entries.name: loadDocumentsForIdsFromValues
entries.signature: loadDocumentsForIdsFromValues(accessor: FirestoreDocumentAccessor<T, D>, values: I[], getId: (value: I) => FirestoreModelId): D[]
entries.description:
Extracts {@link FirestoreModelId}s from arbitrary values and loads them as {@link FirestoreDocument} instances.

Convenience function that maps values through `getId` then delegates to {@link loadDocumentsForIds}.
Requires a full {@link FirestoreDocumentAccessor} (not limited) because loading by ID requires collection context.

params.name: accessor
params.description: - Accessor to load documents with (must have collection context for ID resolution)

params.name: values
params.description: - Source values to extract IDs from.

params.name: getId
params.description: - Extracts a model ID from each value.

entries.returns: Document instances in the same order as the input values.

entries.name: loadDocumentsForIds
entries.signature: loadDocumentsForIds(accessor: FirestoreDocumentAccessor<T, D>, ids: FirestoreModelId[]): D[]

params.name: accessor
params.description: - Accessor to load documents with (must have collection context)

params.name: ids
params.description: - Document IDs within the accessor's collection.

entries.returns: Document instances in the same order as the input IDs.

entries.name: firestoreDocumentLoader
entries.description:
Creates a {@link FirestoreDocumentLoader} from a {@link LimitedFirestoreDocumentAccessorContextExtension}.

The returned loader resolves the appropriate accessor based on whether a transaction is provided,
then delegates to {@link loadDocumentsForDocumentReferences}.

params.name: accessorContext
params.description: - Context that provides accessors for both default and transactional use.

entries.returns: A loader function that converts document references to document instances.

entries.name: firestoreDocumentSnapshotPairsLoader
entries.description:
Creates a {@link FirestoreDocumentSnapshotPairsLoader} from a {@link LimitedFirestoreDocumentAccessorContextExtension}.

The returned loader resolves the appropriate accessor based on whether a transaction is provided,
then maps each snapshot to a {@link FirestoreDocumentSnapshotDataPair} using {@link firestoreDocumentSnapshotPairsLoaderInstance}.

Also satisfies the {@link FirestoreQueryDocumentSnapshotPairsLoader} type since the implementation
handles both {@link DocumentSnapshot} and {@link QueryDocumentSnapshot} inputs.

params.name: accessorContext
params.description: - Context that provides accessors for both default and transactional use.

entries.returns: A loader function that converts snapshots to document-snapshot-data pairs.

entries.name: firestoreDocumentSnapshotPairsLoaderInstance
entries.description:
Creates a {@link FirestoreDocumentSnapshotPairsLoaderInstance} bound to a specific accessor.

The returned function converts a snapshot into a {@link FirestoreDocumentSnapshotDataPair} by:
1. Extracting data with `id`/`key` fields via {@link documentDataWithIdAndKey}
2. Loading the document from the snapshot's reference via `accessor.loadDocument()`
3. Combining all three into a single pair object

params.name: accessor
params.description: - The accessor to bind for document loading.

entries.returns: A reusable function that converts snapshots to pairs using the bound accessor.

entries.name: documentData
entries.signature: documentData(snapshot: DocumentSnapshot<T>, withId?: boolean): Maybe<T> | Maybe<DocumentDataWithIdAndKey<T>>
entries.description:
Extracts data from a {@link DocumentSnapshot}, optionally injecting `id` and `key` fields.

When `withId` is `true` (default is `false`), delegates to {@link documentDataWithIdAndKey} to mutate
the data object with `id` (document ID) and `key` (full document path). When `false`, returns the
raw result of `snapshot.data()`.

For {@link QueryDocumentSnapshot} inputs, the return value is guaranteed non-nullish.
For {@link DocumentSnapshot} inputs, may return `undefined` if the document doesn't exist.

params.name: snapshot
params.description: - The snapshot to extract data from

params.name: withId
params.description: - Whether to inject `id` and `key` fields (default: `false`)

entries.returns: The snapshot data, or `undefined` for non-existent documents

entries.name: documentDataFunction
entries.description:
Returns a data extraction function based on whether `id`/`key` injection is desired.

When `withId` is `true`, returns {@link documentDataWithIdAndKey} (a {@link DocumentDataWithIdAndKeyFunction}).
When `false`, returns a simple `snapshot.data()` wrapper (a {@link DocumentDataFunction}).

Useful for creating reusable mappers when processing arrays of snapshots.

params.name: withId
params.description: - Whether the returned function should inject `id` and `key` fields

entries.returns: A snapshot-to-data extraction function

entries.name: documentDataWithIdAndKey
entries.description:
Extracts data from a {@link DocumentSnapshot} and mutates it to include `id` and `key` fields.

The `id` is set to `snapshot.id` (the document's ID within its collection) and `key` is set
to `snapshot.ref.path` (the full Firestore document path). The mutation is performed in-place
on the data object returned by `snapshot.data()`.

For {@link QueryDocumentSnapshot} inputs, the return value is guaranteed non-nullish.
Returns `undefined` if the document does not exist (i.e. `snapshot.data()` returns falsy).

params.name: snapshot
params.description: - The snapshot to extract and augment data from

entries.returns: The data object with `id` and `key` fields, or `undefined` if the document doesn't exist

entries.name: setIdAndKeyFromSnapshotOnDocumentData
entries.description:
Mutates the input data object to include `id` and `key` fields from a {@link DocumentSnapshot}.

Sets `data.id` to `snapshot.id` and `data.key` to `snapshot.ref.path`. The data object
is modified in-place and also returned for chaining convenience.

params.name: data
params.description: - The data object to augment (mutated in-place)

params.name: snapshot
params.description: - Source of the `id` and `key` values.

entries.returns: The same data object, now typed as {@link DocumentDataWithIdAndKey }

entries.name: setIdAndKeyFromKeyIdRefOnDocumentData
entries.description:
Mutates the input data object to include `id` and `key` fields from a model reference.

Similar to {@link setIdAndKeyFromSnapshotOnDocumentData}, but sources the values from a
{@link FirestoreModelKeyRef} & {@link FirestoreModelIdRef} instead of a snapshot.
The data object is modified in-place and also returned for chaining convenience.

params.name: data
params.description: - The data object to augment (mutated in-place)

params.name: modelRef
params.description: - Source of the `id` and `key` values.

entries.returns: The same data object, now typed as {@link DocumentDataWithIdAndKey }

entries.name: useDocumentSnapshot
entries.signature: useDocumentSnapshot(document: Maybe<D>, use: UseAsync<DocumentSnapshot<FirestoreDocumentData<D>>, O>, defaultValue?: Maybe<AsyncGetterOrValue<O>>): Promise<Maybe<O>>
entries.description:
Fetches a document's snapshot and passes it to a `use` callback, following the {@link UseAsync} pattern.

If `document` is nullish, the `use` callback is not invoked and `defaultValue` is returned instead.
If `document` exists but the snapshot is nullish (shouldn't happen in practice), `defaultValue` is also used.

params.name: document
params.description: - The document to fetch, or nullish to skip.

params.name: use
params.description: - Callback that receives the fetched snapshot and returns a result.

params.name: defaultValue
params.description: - Fallback value when `document` is nullish or the snapshot is unavailable.

entries.returns: The result of `use`, or the default value.

entries.name: useDocumentSnapshotData
entries.signature: useDocumentSnapshotData(): <D extends import("/Users/dereekb/development/git/dbcomponents/packages/firebase/src/lib/common/firestore/accessor/document").FirestoreDocument<any, import("/Users/dereekb/development/git/dbcomponents/packages/firebase/src/lib/common/firestore/collection/collection").FirestoreModelIdentity>, O = void>(document: Maybe<D>, use: UseAsync<import("/Users/dereekb/development/git/dbcomponents/packages/firebase/src/lib/common/firestore/accessor/document").FirestoreDocumentData<D>, O>, defaultValue?: Maybe<AsyncGetterOrValue<O>>) => Promise<Maybe<O>>
entries.description:
Fetches a document's snapshot data (via `snapshot.data()`) and passes it to a `use` callback.

Wraps {@link useDocumentSnapshot} with a mapping step that extracts raw data from the snapshot.
If the document doesn't exist (data is `undefined`), the `use` callback is not invoked
and `defaultValue` is returned instead.
entries.returns: The result of `use`, or the default value

entries.name: firestoreModelIdFromDocument
entries.description:
Extracts the document ID ({@link FirestoreModelId}) from a {@link FirestoreDocument}.

Useful as a mapper function, e.g. `documents.map(firestoreModelIdFromDocument)`.

params.name: document
params.description: - The document to extract the ID from.

entries.returns: The document's ID (the last segment of its Firestore path)

entries.name: firestoreModelIdsFromDocuments
entries.description: Extracts document IDs from an array of {@link FirestoreDocument}s.

params.name: documents
params.description: - Documents to extract IDs from.

entries.returns: Array of document IDs in the same order as the input.

entries.name: firestoreModelKeyFromDocument
entries.description:
Extracts the full Firestore path ({@link FirestoreModelKey}) from a {@link FirestoreDocument}.

Useful as a mapper function, e.g. `documents.map(firestoreModelKeyFromDocument)`.

params.name: document
params.description: - The document to extract the key from.

entries.returns: The document's full Firestore path (e.g. `'users/abc123'`)

entries.name: firestoreModelKeysFromDocuments
entries.description: Extracts full Firestore paths from an array of {@link FirestoreDocument}s.

params.name: documents
params.description: - Documents to extract keys from.

entries.returns: Array of full Firestore paths in the same order as the input.

entries.name: documentReferenceFromDocument
entries.description:
Extracts the {@link DocumentReference} from a {@link FirestoreDocument}.

Useful as a mapper function, e.g. `documents.map(documentReferenceFromDocument)`.

params.name: document
params.description: - The document to extract the reference from.

entries.returns: The underlying Firestore document reference.

entries.name: documentReferencesFromDocuments
entries.description: Extracts {@link DocumentReference}s from an array of {@link FirestoreDocument}s.

params.name: documents
params.description: - Documents to extract references from.

entries.returns: Array of document references in the same order as the input.

entries.name: limitedFirestoreDocumentAccessorSnapshotCache
entries.description:
Creates a {@link LimitedFirestoreDocumentAccessorSnapshotCache} that wraps the given accessor
with an in-memory {@link Map} cache so that repeated loads for the same key return the cached
promise instead of re-reading from Firestore.

The cache stores the promise itself (not the resolved value), which means concurrent requests
for the same key that arrive before the first read completes will also be deduplicated.

The cache lives for the lifetime of the returned object and is never invalidated, so this is
best suited for short-lived scopes (e.g. a single request or batch operation) where stale reads
are acceptable.

params.name: accessor
params.description: - The accessor to wrap with caching behavior.

entries.returns: A {@link LimitedFirestoreDocumentAccessorSnapshotCache } backed by the given accessor.
